calls.note: Held at a 25% loss. Underperformance likely for the next two to three quarters, but among large-cap IT, TCS is adapting AI technologies relatively quickly and valuations are close to its historical long-term averages - so hold. He added the mailer should have decided upfront whether the position was for trading or investing.

Hold only with a high risk appetite. The IPO season cooling will hit margins, but the company runs 50-60% operating margins and is run by Dharmesh Mehta, one of the market masters in investment banking and lining up IPOs. If a foreign company acquires a stake, there is scope to outperform a lot in the long term in Indian investment banking.
Holder at 3,520 - be a bit alert. Valuations look stretched, the stock gets discounted a lot, and speculative activity comes and goes in the medium-to-short term, so keep running profits selectively.
Holder at a 25% loss - no averaging at this time. It's a dark horse. Hold for now and exit into rallies when they come.
